1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the command of God our Saviour and of Christ Jesus our hope.
To Timothy, my true son in the faith: May God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord give you grace, mercy, and peace.
When I went to Macedonia, I asked you to stay in Ephesus. I wanted you to order certain people to stop teaching false things,
and to stop paying attention to made-up stories and endless family-tree lists. These only stir up arguments instead of doing God's work, which is done by faith.
The goal of this command is love — love that comes from a pure heart, a good conscience, and an honest faith.
Some people have wandered away from these things and turned aside to empty talk.
They want to be teachers of the law, but they do not understand what they are saying or what they so confidently claim.
We know that the law is good, if a person uses it the right way.

We also know that the law is not made for those who are good, but for those who break the law and refuse to obey, who are against God and full of sin, who have nothing holy about them, who kill their fathers or mothers, who murder,

who sin sexually, men who have sexual relations with other men, those who steal people to sell them as slaves, liars, those who make false promises, and whoever else does what is against sound teaching.
This teaching agrees with the glorious good news of the blessed God, the good news he trusted to me.
I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who gave me strength, because he counted me trustworthy and chose me to serve him.
In the past I said evil things against him, I attacked him, and I was cruel. But God showed me mercy, because I acted in ignorance and unbelief.
The grace of our Lord poured over me, together with the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
Here is a true saying that everyone should accept: Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners — and I am the worst of them.

But for this very reason God showed me mercy: so that in me, the worst sinner, Christ Jesus could show how very patient he is, as an example to those who would later believe in him and receive eternal life.
Now to the King who lives forever, who never dies and cannot be seen, the only God, be honor and glory forever and ever. Amen.
Timothy, my son, I give you this command, in keeping with the prophecies once spoken about you. Use them to fight the good fight,
holding on to faith and a good conscience. Some people have rejected these and have wrecked their faith.
Among them are Hymenaeus and Alexander, whom I have handed over to Satan to be taught not to speak against God.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: First of all, then, I urge that requests, prayers, and thanks be made for everyone:
for kings and for all who are in charge, so that we can live peaceful and quiet lives, in a way that honors God and is good and honest.
This is good, and it pleases God our Saviour,
who wants everyone to be saved and to come to know the truth.
For there is one God and one go-between for God and people, the man Christ Jesus,
who gave himself as the price to set everyone free. This was shown at the right time.
And for this I was made a preacher and an apostle — I am telling the truth, I am not lying — a teacher of the true faith to the other nations.
So I want the men everywhere to pray, lifting up holy hands, without anger or arguing.
In the same way, I want the women to dress in a proper and sensible way, with modest clothing. They should not depend on fancy hairstyles, gold, pearls, or expensive clothes,
but on good deeds, which are right for women who say they worship God.
A woman should learn quietly and humbly.
I do not allow a woman to teach or to have authority over a man; she should be quiet.
For Adam was made first, and then Eve.
And Adam was not the one who was fooled; the woman was fooled and fell into sin.
But women will be kept safe through having children, if they continue in faith, love, and holiness, with good sense.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Here is a true saying: anyone who wants to be a church overseer wants a good work.
An overseer must have nothing against him. He must be faithful to his wife, level-headed, sensible, respectable, welcoming to strangers, and able to teach.
He must not be a heavy drinker or violent, but gentle. He must not be someone who argues or loves money.
He must manage his own family well, and his children must obey him and treat him with respect.
(If a man does not know how to manage his own family, how can he take care of God's church?)
He must not be a new believer, or he may become proud and fall under the same judgment as the devil.
He must also have a good name among outsiders, so that he does not fall into shame and the devil's trap.
In the same way, deacons must be worthy of respect, sincere, not heavy drinkers, and not greedy for dishonest money.
They must hold on to the deep truth of the faith with a clean conscience.
They must first be tested, and if there is nothing against them, then let them serve as deacons.
In the same way, the women must be worthy of respect. They must not tell lies about others; they must be sensible and trustworthy in everything.
A deacon must be faithful to his wife and must manage his children and his household well.
Those who serve well as deacons gain a good standing and great confidence in their faith in Christ Jesus.
I am writing these things to you, even though I hope to come to you soon.
But if I am delayed, you will know how people should behave in God's household, which is the church of the living God, the pillar and foundation of the truth.

Without question, the secret of our faith is great: Christ appeared in a human body, was shown to be right by the Spirit, was seen by angels, was preached among the nations, was believed in around the world, and was taken up in glory.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: The Spirit clearly says that in later times some people will turn away from the faith. They will follow lying spirits and teachings that come from demons.
Such teachings come from liars who pretend to be sincere, whose consciences have been burned and made numb.
They tell people not to marry and not to eat certain foods. But God created these foods to be received with thanks by those who believe and know the truth.
Everything God created is good, and nothing should be refused if it is received with thanks,
because it is made holy by the word of God and by prayer.
If you point these things out to the brothers and sisters, you will be a good servant of Christ Jesus, fed by the words of the faith and the good teaching you have followed.
Have nothing to do with godless, silly stories. Instead, train yourself to be godly.
Training the body has some value, but being godly has value for everything. It holds promise both for this life and for the life to come.
Here is a true saying that everyone should accept.
This is why we work hard and struggle: because we have put our hope in the living God, who is the Saviour of all people, and especially of those who believe.
Command and teach these things.
Do not let anyone look down on you because you are young. Instead, set an example for the believers in your speech, your life, your love, your faith, and your purity.
Until I come, give your attention to reading the Scriptures to the church, to encouraging people, and to teaching.
Do not neglect the gift that is in you, the gift that was given to you through prophecy when the elders laid their hands on you.
Practice these things and give yourself fully to them, so that everyone can see your progress.
Watch your life and your teaching closely. Keep at it, because if you do, you will save both yourself and those who hear you.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Do not speak harshly to an older man. Instead, encourage him as you would your father. Treat younger men as brothers,
older women as mothers, and younger women as sisters, with complete purity.
Take care of widows who are truly alone.
But if a widow has children or grandchildren, they should first learn to show their faith by caring for their own family and paying back their parents and grandparents, for this pleases God.
The widow who is truly alone, with no one to help, puts her hope in God and keeps on praying and asking God for help, night and day.
But the widow who lives only for pleasure is dead even while she is alive.
Give the people these instructions, so that no one can find fault with them.
Anyone who does not provide for his own relatives, and especially for his own family, has turned away from the faith and is worse than someone who does not believe at all.
A widow should be put on the list of widows only if she is at least sixty years old and was faithful to her husband.
She must be known for her good deeds: bringing up children, welcoming strangers, washing the feet of God's people, helping those in trouble, and giving herself to every good work.
But do not put younger widows on the list. For when their desires pull them away from Christ, they will want to marry.
Then they bring judgment on themselves, because they have broken their first promise.
Besides, they get into the habit of being lazy and going from house to house. And they do not only become lazy, but also gossips and busybodies, saying things they should not.
So I would rather that younger widows marry, have children, and manage their homes, giving the enemy no reason to speak against us.
For some have already turned away to follow Satan.
If any woman who is a believer has widows in her family, she should help them, so that the church is not burdened, and can help the widows who are truly alone.
The elders who lead well should be given double honor, especially those whose work is preaching and teaching.
For the Scripture says: Do not stop the ox from eating while it treads out the grain, and: A worker deserves his pay.
Do not accept an accusation against an elder unless it is brought by two or three witnesses.
Those who keep on sinning should be corrected in front of everyone, so that the others will be warned.
Before God, before Christ Jesus, and before the chosen angels, I tell you to follow these instructions without favoring anyone or treating people unfairly.
Do not be too quick to lay your hands on anyone, and do not share in the sins of others. Keep yourself pure.
Stop drinking only water. Use a little wine because of your stomach and your frequent illnesses.
The sins of some people are clear, and lead the way to judgment; the sins of others show up later.
In the same way, good deeds are clear, and even those that are not cannot stay hidden.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: All who are slaves should treat their masters with full respect, so that no one will speak evil of God's name and our teaching.

Those who have masters who are believers should not show them less respect just because they are brothers. Instead, they should serve them even better, because those who benefit from their service are believers whom they love. Teach and urge these things.
Suppose someone teaches something different and does not agree with the sound words of our Lord Jesus Christ and with godly teaching.
That person is proud and understands nothing. He has an unhealthy love for arguments and word-fights, which lead to jealousy, fighting, insults, evil suspicions,
and constant friction between people whose minds are corrupt and who have lost the truth. They think that being godly is a way to get rich. Stay away from people like that.
But being godly, together with being content, is great gain.
For we brought nothing into the world, and we can take nothing out of it.
So if we have food and clothing, we will be content with that.
But people who want to get rich fall into temptation and a trap, and into many foolish and harmful desires that drag them down to ruin and destruction.
For the love of money is a root of all kinds of evil. Some people, wanting money, have wandered away from the faith and caused themselves much pain.
But you, man of God, run away from all this. Instead, go after right living, godliness, faith, love, patience, and gentleness.
Fight the good fight of faith. Take hold of the eternal life to which you were called when you made your good confession in front of many witnesses.
Before God, who gives life to everything, and before Christ Jesus, who made the good confession when he stood before Pontius Pilate, I charge you:
keep this command without stain or blame until our Lord Jesus Christ appears.
God will bring this about at the right time. He is the blessed and only Ruler, the King of kings and Lord of lords.
He alone can never die, and he lives in light that no one can come near. No one has ever seen him, and no one can. To him be honor and power forever. Amen.

Command those who are rich in this world not to be proud and not to put their hope in money, which is so uncertain. Instead, they should put their hope in God, who richly gives us everything to enjoy.
Tell them to do good, to be rich in good deeds, and to be generous and ready to share.
In this way they store up treasure for themselves, a good foundation for the future, so that they may take hold of the life that is truly life.
Timothy, guard what has been trusted to you. Stay away from godless, empty talk and from the arguments of what is falsely called “knowledge.”
Some people who claim to have this knowledge have wandered away from the faith. May grace be with you all. Amen.
